title: solanum plousianthemum var. rhodesianum
intro: Solanum plousianthemum var. rhodesianum, also known as the Rhodesian wild potato or bitter apple, is a plant species within the nightshade family, Solanaceae. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to act as an anthelmintic, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory agent. The health benefits of this plant are attributed to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to reduce inflammation, improve immune function, and alleviate symptoms of various health conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and skin conditions.
